message:
🌱 Add keycloak: Broken (read-only) database connections not getting removed mission (#991)

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,73 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "keycloak-24493-broken-read-only-database-connections-not-getting-removed-from-co",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "keycloak: Broken (read-only) database connections not getting removed from connection pool, keycloak claims to be healthy.",
+    "description": "Broken (read-only) database connections not getting removed from connection pool, keycloak claims to be healthy. When using PostgreSQL Aurora Multi-AZ, read-only connections linger in the connection pool after a failover, causing INSERT failures while Keycloak still reports healthy. This issue affects 23+ users.",
+    "type": "troubleshoot",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify the broken connection pool symptoms",
+        "description": "Check your Keycloak logs for read-only transaction errors after a database failover:\n```bash\n# If running in a container:\ndocker logs keycloak 2>&1 | grep -i 'read-only transaction'\n\n# Or check the Keycloak version:\ndocker exec keycloak /opt/keycloak/bin/kc.sh show-config\n```\nLook for error: `cannot execute INSERT in a read-only transaction`\n\nThis indicates the connection pool is still holding stale connections to a former primary that is now a read-only replica."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Understand the root cause",
+        "description": "After a PostgreSQL Aurora Multi-AZ failover, connections to the old primary become read-only. Keycloak's Agroal connection pool does not detect these broken connections and continues to report healthy status via its health check endpoint.\n\nThe root cause is that PostgreSQL (Aurora) eagerly evaluates constant expressions like `SELECT 1/0`, so the standard connection validation query does not properly detect read-only connections."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the connection validation fix",
+        "description": "The fix uses a connection validation SQL query that detects read-only replicas by leveraging `pg_is_in_recovery()`:\n```sql\nSELECT 1 / CASE WHEN pg_is_in_recovery() THEN 0 ELSE 1 END\n```\nThis query succeeds on the primary (writable) node and throws a division-by-zero error on read-only replicas, forcing the connection pool to discard stale connections.\n\nConfigure the validation query in your Keycloak database settings (e.g., via `KC_DB_URL` parameters or Quarkus datasource configuration)."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Confirm the connection pool issue is resolved",
+        "description": "Verify the fix by simulating or waiting for a failover event:\n1. Check Keycloak logs for the absence of `cannot execute INSERT in a read-only transaction`\n2. Verify the health endpoint returns healthy status only when the database is truly writable\n3. Monitor the connection pool for stale connection removal after failovers"
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "PostgreSQL Aurora eagerly evaluates constant expressions, so the standard connection validation query could not detect read-only replicas. The fix uses `SELECT 1 / CASE WHEN pg_is_in_recovery() THEN 0 ELSE 1 END` as the validation query, which fails on read-only replicas and forces the connection pool to discard stale connections after a failover.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "SELECT 1 / CASE WHEN pg_is_in_recovery() THEN 0 ELSE 1 END"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "keycloak",
+      "community",
+      "identity",
+      "troubleshoot"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "keycloak"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [],
+    "difficulty": "intermediate",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "troubleshoot"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "community",
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "issue": "https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/24493",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak"
+    },
+    "reactions": 23,
+    "comments": 18,
+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "tools": [
+      "docker"
+    ],
+    "description": "A running Keycloak server (via Docker/Podman or Kubernetes) with a PostgreSQL Aurora Multi-AZ database backend."
+  },
+  "security": {
+    "scannedAt": "2026-03-13T06:48:42.728Z",

+    "sanitized": true,

+  }
+}
